items.name: LifecycleManager
items.description: Graceful shutdown manager for zero-http applications. Tracks active connections, drains in-flight requests, closes WebSocket and SSE connections, and shuts down ORM databases before exiting.

methods.description: Register a lifecycle event listener.
Remove a lifecycle event listener.
Track an active HTTP request. Called automatically by the App request handler when lifecycle management is enabled.
Number of currently active HTTP requests.
Register a WebSocket pool for graceful shutdown. All connections in registered pools are closed with code `1001` during shutdown.
Unregister a WebSocket pool.
Track an active SSE stream for graceful shutdown.
Register an ORM Database instance for graceful shutdown. The database connection is closed during shutdown.
methods.example:
const db = new Database({ adapter: 'sqlite', file: ':memory:' });
  app.registerDatabase(db);

examples.code:
const db = new Database({ adapter: 'sqlite', file: ':memory:' });
  app.registerDatabase(db);

methods.description: Unregister an ORM Database instance.
Install `SIGTERM` and `SIGINT` process signal handlers that trigger graceful shutdown. Called automatically by `app.listen()`. Safe to call multiple times — handlers are only installed once.
Remove previously installed signal handlers. Called automatically during shutdown cleanup.

Perform a full graceful shutdown. Shutdown sequence: 1. Emit `'beforeShutdown'` — run pre-shutdown hooks (flush metrics, etc.) 2. Stop accepting new connections (server.close) 3. Close all WebSocket connections with code `1001` (Going Away) 4. Close all SSE streams 5. Wait for in-flight HTTP requests to complete (with timeout) 6. Close all registered ORM database connections 7. Emit `'shutdown'` — final cleanup complete If in-flight requests do not complete within the configured timeout (default 30s), they are forcefully terminated.

methodOptions.notes: Maximum ms to wait for in-flight requests. Overrides the configured default.

methods.description: Whether the server is currently draining (rejecting new requests).
Whether the server has fully shut down.

items.name: ClusterManager
items.description: Clustering support for zero-http applications. Forks worker processes, manages automatic restarts with backoff, and provides IPC messaging between the primary and workers.

examples.title: With Options

options.notes: Number of worker processes (default: CPU count).

methods.description: Whether the current process is the primary (master) process.
Whether the current process is a worker process.
Number of configured workers.
Get all active worker IDs.
Number of currently alive workers.
Fork all worker processes. Only call from the primary process.
Send a typed message from the primary to all workers.
Send a typed message to a specific worker.
Send a typed message from a worker to the primary process. Call this from within a worker process.
Register a handler for a typed IPC message. On the primary, receives messages from workers. On workers, receives messages from the primary.

methodParams.notes: Handler `(data, worker?) => void`. `worker` is only present on the primary.

methods.description: Enable automatic per-worker metrics aggregation. Workers periodically send their metrics snapshot to the primary, which merges them into a single registry for exposition.

methodParams.notes: Registry to aggregate into (on primary) or report from (on worker).

methods.description: Stop the per-worker metrics reporting timer.

Enable sticky sessions by hashing client IP addresses to specific workers. Ensures WebSocket and SSE connections from the same client always land on the same worker for proper room/state management. Must be called on the primary BEFORE listen(). Replaces the default round-robin OS scheduling with a custom `connection` listener that distributes sockets to workers based on IP hash.

methodParams.notes: The HTTP server to attach to.

methods.description: Perform a rolling restart of all workers (zero-downtime). Workers are restarted one at a time — a new worker is spawned and confirmed listening before the old one is disconnected.

methodReturns.description: Resolves when all workers have been replaced.

methods.description: Shut down the entire cluster gracefully. Sends `'shutdown'` IPC message to all workers, then waits for them to exit. Workers that don't exit within the timeout are killed.

methodOptions.notes: Maximum ms to wait for workers to exit.

methodReturns.description: Resolves when all workers have exited.

methods.description: High-level clustering helper. Forks workers on the primary process and runs the provided setup function on each worker.

methodParams.notes:
Function to execute on each worker process.
Receives the ClusterManager instance as argument.

methodReturns.description: The cluster manager instance (on both primary and workers).
